name: drf-appkit

metadata.name: drf-appkit
metadata.summary: A Django app providing generic CRUD functionality

documentation:
# drf-appkit

### Build: 
`python3 -m build`

### Deploy to PyPi:
`python3 -m twine upload dist/*`

classes.name: appkit.drf.mixins.DestroyModelMixin
documentation:
Override of DRF implementation in rest_framework/mixins.
In the event that a record can not be deleted due to a protected foreign key,
it is preferable to construct a standard error response rather than
simply bomb with a django exception.
See: https://github.com/encode/django-rest-framework/issues/5958

functions.name: appkit.auth.get_userprofile_model
documentation: Return the UserProfile model that is active in this project.

methods.name: appkit.drf.filters.SearchFilter.get_search_terms
documentation:
Overridden to support the situation where we wish to search for an
exact phrase (supplied as a quoted string)

functions.name: appkit.transformers.strtobool
documentation:
Convert a string representation of truth to true (1) or false (0).

True values are 'y', 'yes', 't', 'true', 'on', and '1'; false values
are 'n', 'no', 'f', 'false', 'off', and '0'.  Raises ValueError if
'val' is anything else.

modules.name: appkit.auth.permissions
documentation:
This permission system piggybacks on top of the Django Permission system.
Django requires that we associate permissions with specific models. Django also
requires that permissions have a single name, and the namespace is given by the
app name of the model.

This is not flexible enough for our needs.  Rather, we will associate all of our
permissions with the app.models.UserProfile (as that is who this permission system governs)
and we will make a new naming scheme which can be mapped to django's permission name
scheme.

Also, django's permission model's name field is only 50 characters long which is
totally ridiculous. So we will modify that here to be 255 characters which is much
more reasonable.

classes.name: appkit.auth.permissions.PermissionError
documentation: Raised when a permission is misconfigured.

classes.name: appkit.auth.permissions.PermissionDoesNotExist
documentation: Raised when a permission does not exist

classes.name: appkit.auth.permissions.Permission

methods.name: appkit.auth.permissions.Permission.get_allowed_users_Q
documentation: Returns a Q object that identifies the users for this permission.

methods.name: appkit.auth.permissions.Permission.get_allowed_users
documentation:
Returns the set of employees that this permission gains access
to for a given purpose.

methods.name: appkit.auth.permissions.Permission.__nonzero__
documentation: Allow these objects to be used in a boolean context.

methods.name: appkit.auth.permissions.Permission.users
documentation:
Gets all users that have this permission assigned to their user,
or belong to a group with this permission

methods.name: appkit.auth.permissions.Permission.users_and_groups
documentation:
Gets the set of users that hold this permission either through
direct assignment or membership to one or more groups that have it.

methods.name: appkit.auth.permissions.Permission.user_app_permissions
documentation:
Gets all of the app permissions that a user has.

This includes all of the permission they have directly assigned to them
and all of the permissions they have via a group. It returns it as a
list of tuples where each tuple contains the permission itself,
followed by the list of groups that give the permission.

methods.name: appkit.auth.permissions.Permission.user_non_app_permissions
documentation:
Gets all of the NON-app permissions that a user has.

This includes all of the permission they have directly assigned to them
and all of the permissions they have via a group. It returns it as a
list of tuples where each tuple contains the permission itself,
followed by the list of groups that give the permission.

methods.name: appkit.auth.permissions.Permission.all_permissions
documentation:
Gets ALL of the permissions that a user has.

This includes all of the permission they have directly assigned to them
and all of the permissions they have via a group.
Represents a custom permission in the django subsystem.

classes.name: appkit.auth.permissions.PermissionSet

methods.name: appkit.auth.permissions.PermissionSet.__nonzero__
documentation: Allow these objects to be used in a boolean context.

methods.name: appkit.auth.permissions.PermissionSet.get_allowed_users_Q
documentation: Returns a Q objects which identifies the users for this permission.

methods.name: appkit.auth.permissions.PermissionSet.get_allowed_users
documentation:
Returns the set of employees that this permission gains access
to for a given purpose.

Represents a set of permissions which are permissable for access
to a given system.

When you pass in the permissions, pass them in from the most permissive
to the least permissive. All of these methods are eager, in that they will
stop processing as soon as they find the first permission the user has
from the given set.

functions.name: appkit.util.string_with_unique_suffix
documentation:
Args:
    value: The string to generate a unique value for
    haystack: The set of strings to compare against for uniqueness

Returns: The given value with a suffix index that renders it unique among the given set

functions.name: appkit.util.split_path
documentation:
Given a path, return a tuple containing the specified number of initial elements
and the remainder, which may be none.  Raises ValueError if the given path does
not contain the specified number of components.

e.g.

  > split_path('yabba/dabba/doo')
  ('yabba', 'dabba/doo')

  > split_path('yabba/dabba/doo', count=2)
  ('yabba', 'dabba', 'doo')

  > split_path('yabba.dabba.doo', '.', 3)
  ('yabba', 'dabba', 'doo', None)

functions.name: appkit.util.value_for_key_path
documentation: Provide access to a property chain

functions.name: appkit.util.primitive_attribute_names
documentation:
Given a django model class, return a list containing the names of all
primitive fields (ex: strings, numbers, dates, booleans, etc).

A primitive attribute is any field that _does not_ represent a relationship
or complex data structure (ex: JSONField)

classes.name: appkit.util.UploadTo
documentation:
This class is used to generate a unique directory for uploaded files so as
to avoid having to rename the uploaded file due to a name collision.
An instance of this class may be supplied as the 'upload_to' parameter of
a FileField whereby the given media_path_prefix is a subdirectory under the
media root directory (ex: 'attachments', 'avatars', etc).

methods.name: appkit.api.views.MasterDetailViewSet.filter_list_queryset
documentation:
The base MasterDetailViewSet performs no filtering at all.
Subclasses may override this method to further limit the
queryset based on the context of the given request.

methods.name: appkit.api.views.MasterDetailViewSet.restrict_list_queryset
documentation:
The base MasterDetailViewSet performs no restriction at all.
Subclasses may override this method to further limit the
queryset based on the context of the given request.

Base class to provide defaults and behavior
that is common across all ModelViewSets

methods.name: appkit.api.tests.TestCaseAssertionMixin.assertReturnListContains
documentation:
Helper method to test whether a given ReturnList
(typically generated in response to an API request)
 contains a given record.
 Test is based on whether or not a record in the given
 return_list has an ID equal to the given record.

methods.name: appkit.api.tests.TestCaseAssertionMixin.assertNotReturnListContains
documentation:
Helper method to test whether a given ReturnList
(typically generated in response to an API request)
 contains a given record.
 Test is based on whether or not a record in the given
 return_list has an ID equal to the given record.

A mixin to provide additional test assertions that specifically applicable
to django-rest-framework responses.
